If you’re a big fan of horror films, you’ll definitely want to watch our latest movie, The Shadow Killer. Now, not only can you see it for free but you’ll also get a treat of popcorn and coke as well as a limited edition The Shadow Killer T-shirt. All you have to do is log on to our website, leopardstudio.com, and sign up for the preview. P.S. 1. The Shadow Killer is an R-rated film, so its preview is only open to adults.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 111-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: From the second paragraph of this essay, we can know that.
options: higher taxes may cause more wives to refuse to give birth to children
the more tax money government gets, the more couples want children
improved social welfare is something both America and Europe share

whether or not couples want to raise children depends on social welfare ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 The guide dog, or the seeing-eye dog, is a dog trained to help blind people. Its main tasks include helping them get from one place to another, leading them around various obstacles on their way, etc. In most countries nowadays, it has been granted access to restaurants, public transportation stations or vehicles, and office buildings, where there is often a strict no-pet-animal policy. th The origin of the guide dog has remained unclear. Some say that it can date back to the 16 century, while others claim that the practice of using dogs to help blind people already existed in ancient Roman times. On the other hand, we do know that during World War I, the Germans first founded a training school for dogs as well as other animals to help soldiers blinded on the battlefield. In 1927, the story of this training school was reported in The Saturday Evening Post, a major newspaper in the U.S. From then on, the Americans began to train dogs, pairing them with blind people as their helpers. Of course, not all dogs are up to par when it comes to being selected as guide dogs. There are several requirements they must meet, such as perfect health, a steady temperament, and a proper size. Labradors, golden retrievers, and German shepherds are the breeds of dogs most likely to be chosen to work as guide dogs. Once a dog (usually a puppy) is chosen as a guide dog, it will go through at least 18 months of hard and expensive training before it is officially put to work. Moreover, while a guide dog is working, it has to stay focused at all times. That is, the last thing it needs is any distraction from outside. Hence, the next time you see a guide dog at work, be sure to avoid patting, feeding, or talking to it, no matter how adorable it may look.
questions.explanation: 正解：(D)；來源：111-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf
